summary: The packet preserves generic evidence labels but overstates acceptance readiness from partial support.

findings.title: Acceptance claim exceeds synthetic evidence
findings.summary: The packet says it can be accepted without follow-up even though Evidence label C is partial and caller-owned.
findings.recommendation: Revise the claim or add direct supporting evidence before treating it as accepted.
evidence: The evidence is partial and should stay caveated.

findings.title: Caveat does not preserve caller-owned thresholds
findings.summary: The downstream-action caveat names caller ownership, but the claim still implies sufficient caveats without proof-threshold evidence.
findings.recommendation: Keep the caveat explicit and route threshold decisions to the embedding workflow.
evidence: The caller still owns proof thresholds and routing.

decision.summary: The packet is usable as a contract exercise after preserving follow-up language for the support gap.

non_blocking_findings.title: Acceptance claim exceeds synthetic evidence

non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Revise the claim or add direct supporting evidence before treating it as accepted.
evidence: The evidence is partial and should stay caveated.

non_blocking_findings.title: Caveat does not preserve caller-owned thresholds
non_blocking_findings.summary: The downstream-action caveat names caller ownership, but the claim still implies sufficient caveats without proof-threshold evidence.
non_blocking_findings.recommendation: Keep the caveat explicit and route threshold decisions to the embedding workflow.
evidence: The caller still owns proof thresholds and routing.

follow_up: Collect direct support or revise the acceptance claim.
Track whether caller-owned thresholds remain explicit in downstream use.

provenance.label: Privacy boundary
provenance.value: No private source names, local paths, tracker keys, or account details

markdown:
Output classification: review

The packet is acceptable as a contract exercise only with follow-up. Evidence label C is partial, so the acceptance claim should stay caveated and caller-owned.
